summary.intent: Iterate on PR #686 (ars-ui clipboard component) through multiple Codex review cycles, fixing accessibility and correctness issues raised in each pass until the reviewer approves.
summary.outcome: Two full Codex review cycles completed: first pass issues (aria-labelledby, aria-label, missing Vec import) were fixed and resolved; second pass surfaced a missing-callback bug where WriteText with no on_copy adapter left the state machine stuck in Copying — fixed by emitting CopyError(ApiUnavailable) as a fallback.

repo: The repo is fogodev/ars-ui, a Rust UI component library using a state-machine architecture for components like Clipboard.
Codex review is triggered by posting '@codex review' as a PR comment and polled via GitHub reactions (👀 = in-progress, +1 = approved) and unresolved review threads.
The project uses cargo insta for snapshot testing, cargo xtask spec validate for spec conformance, and a spec markdown file at spec/components/specialized/clipboard.md.
Clipboard component state machine lives in crates/ars-components/src/specialized/clipboard/mod.rs and uses Events like CopyError(ApiUnavailable) to transition out of the Copying state.
Props::on_copy is an Option — when None, the WriteText effect is a no-op, which previously caused the machine to hang in Copying state indefinitely.

code.finding: The write-text effect handler needed a guard: if on_copy callback is None, immediately send CopyError(ApiUnavailable) rather than awaiting a completion that will never arrive.
Spec snippet required updating to document the ApiUnavailable error path when no adapter callback is wired.

workflow: Codex review threads can be resolved programmatically via GitHub GraphQL mutation resolveReviewThread with the thread's node ID.
Polling loop pattern used: check reactions and unresolved Codex thread count every 60s to detect review state transitions without manual intervention.
Reply to each review thread with the fixing commit SHA before resolving — provides traceability linking issue to fix.
Commit message includes 'Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>' to credit the AI reviewer.

friction: Polling the Codex review required a busy-wait loop with 60s sleeps — no webhook or event-driven mechanism available.
Thread IDs for resolution had to be hardcoded from a prior API call, requiring two separate API round-trips (fetch threads, then resolve by ID).
The second Codex pass took multiple minutes with several inconclusive polls before surfacing the new finding.
open_items: A third '@codex review' pass was triggered after the missing-callback fix commit but the transcript ends before its outcome is known — approval or further findings pending.
The clipboard spec markdown was updated but broader spec validation (cargo xtask spec validate) results are not shown — may need verification.
No CI/CD pipeline status shown — full test suite and lint results across the whole repo not confirmed green.
